div.p: No official record of the substance of this meeting has been found, and the only reference to it appears in Hopkins’ notes of January 24, post, p. 839. According to Hopkins, Macmillan brought news that de Gaulle insisted on the dominant role in the French movement. After conferring with Roosevelt, Hopkins informed Macmillan that it was up to Churchill to bring de Gaulle to a meeting with Giraud.
